page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-24-lssd is a memory-optimized server powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor with x86_64 architecture. It features 24 dedicated vCPUs across 12 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The system is equipped with 186 GB of RAM, providing 7.75 GB of memory per core, and includes a massive 504 MB L3 cache. Storage is bundled with 1343 GB of local NVMe SSD capacity across four drives, while no GPUs are present.

Benchmark results indicate exceptional single-core performance, with Geekbench single-core and text processing scores reaching the top 10% tier. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also rank in the top tier. Multi-core performance is strong, placing in the top 25% for Geekbench multi-core and bzip3 decompression. Memory performance is robust, with Passmark memory scores in the top 25%, though uncached large-block read and write memory bandwidth remains average. For specialized workloads, the CPU's Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) support strong prompt processing and text generation for small LLM models, though performance drops to the bottom 10% for large 70B parameter model text generation.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the high memory-to-vCPU ratio combined with fast local NVMe SSD storage provides a dense resource profile for memory-bound and I/O-intensive workloads. The inclusion of dedicated compute resources and local SSDs eliminates the need for external storage attachments for high-throughput tasks. This configuration is highly suited for database operations, in-memory caching, static web serving, software compilation, and text processing pipelines.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-24-lssd is a memory-optimized server configured with 24 dedicated vCPUs and 186 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor. It features 1343 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a large 504 MB L3 cache, with no GPU accelerators. The server exhibits top-tier single-core performance, particularly in text processing and single-threaded compression benchmarks, alongside strong multi-core capabilities. While memory bandwidth and database operations perform at average levels, the system delivers high static web serving throughput and strong small-model LLM inference. This hardware profile offers high resource density, making it a cost-effective choice for memory-intensive applications without requiring external storage. It is highly suited for in-memory databases, web serving, and text processing workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-24-lssd is a memory-optimized server featuring 24 vCPUs, 186 GB RAM, and 1343 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier single-core and strong web serving performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-24-lssd features 24 vCPUs, 186 GB RAM, and 1343 GB NVMe SSD storage, optimized for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory and local NVMe storage configuration optimized for high-throughput text processing, web serving, and database workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor with 24 dedicated vCPUs and 186 GB RAM
Bundled 1343 GB local NVMe SSD storage across four drives
Top-tier single-core performance in text processing and compression
Strong static web serving throughput and small-model LLM inference
Well-suited for in-memory databases, caching, and text processing pipelines
